summary: Iter 15 verified end-to-end. Backend: 15/15 pytest passed (live Margaret decode 47s, deterministic Rule 14/15 helper unit tests, prompt presence for Rules 11/12/13, paid-tool gating, daily-limit 429). Frontend: Statement Decoder embed daily-limit shows friendly string and does NOT crash React (previous object-detail bug fixed); Login wrong-password shows friendly 'Invalid email or password' toast with form still rendered. extractErrorMessage helper is present with correct signature and used by all 14 call sites verified via grep. Note: live LLM produced 3H/3M/5L = 11 anomalies vs requested 4H/4M/4L = 12 (LLM non-determinism on Margaret fixture — RULE_7 AT-HM stream miscoding slot landed under RULE_4 this run; RULE_14 LLM-side flagged STATEMENT_PERIOD_ACCURACY even though deterministic variant correctly did not fire for 30-day period). This does not affect any passing assertion because tests now check anomaly_count == length-of-anomalies (post-merge contract) rather than hard-coded 4/4/4.

minor.issue: LLM-driven RULE_14 STATEMENT_PERIOD_ACCURACY fired on a 30-day April 1-30 span for Margaret fixture; prompt says 'flag when span > 35 days', so LLM is being over-eager (false positive LOW). Deterministic RULE_14_PERIOD_PARSE_WARNING correctly did NOT fire. Consider tightening the LLM prompt or relying solely on the deterministic variant.
LLM output variance on Margaret — iter14 baseline had RULE_7 in highs but this run collapsed it into RULE_4. Functional but non-deterministic. Consider pinning temperature to 0.

action_items: Consider tightening AUDITOR_SYSTEM so LLM does not emit the RULE_14 LLM-side variant on <=35 day spans (deterministic helper already handles it correctly).
Optional: pin LLM temperature=0 on the auditor chat to stabilise anomaly_count regressions across runs.
critical_code_review_comments: extractErrorMessage helper (lines 9-25 of /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js) is clean, side-effect-free, handles string/object/missing detail correctly — good defensive fix.
_add_parse_warnings (agents.py ~L899-975) correctly dedupes against existing rule keys and runs on both success and fallback paths (_empty_audit).
agents.py is 976 lines — approaching the 700-line threshold flagged in code_review_guidelines. Consider splitting the extractor/auditor prompts and helpers into separate modules.

Margaret regression fixture has an intentional gross-total inconsistency (reported 2429.49 vs summed line items ~2513.49). Good — exercises RULE_15. Document this clearly in the fixture file header to avoid future confusion.
test_credentials: cathy@example.com / testpass123 (valid; used wrong password to test 401 toast)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /tmp/decoded_public_iter15.json as cached live decode (reported_total_gross=2429.49, period 2026-04-01 -> 2026-04-30, at_hm_commitments=[], 11 anomalies this run). /app/backend/tests/test_iter15_rules.py is the regression suite for Rules 11-15; deterministic helper tests run without LLM and are the stable contract. Live-decode fixture tests tolerate LLM variance by asserting anomaly_count equals length-of-anomalies, not hard-coded numbers.
rca of the issue: No bug this iteration — iter14's React crash on object-detail (daily_limit) is confirmed fixed in StatementDecoderEmbed.jsx via typed-branch catch. All toast.error sites switched to extractErrorMessage so any future endpoint that returns a structured detail object will render safely.
